1: And when Balaam saw that it pleased the LORD LORD to bless Israel, he went not, as at other times, to seek for enchantments, but he set his face toward the wilderness.
2: And Balaam lifted up his eyes, and he saw Israel abiding in his tents in his tents according to their tribes; and the spirit of God came upon him.
3: And the man whose eyes are open hath said:
4: Falling into a trance into a trance , but having his eyes open:
5: And And thy tabernacles, O Israel!
6: And And as cedar trees beside the waters.
7: And his kingdom shall be exalted.
8: And pierce them them through with his arrows.
9: And cursed is is he that curseth thee.
10: And Balak's anger was kindled against Balaam, and he smote his hands together: and Balak said unto Balaam, I called thee to curse mine enemies, and, behold, thou hast altogether blessed them them these three times.
11: Therefore now flee thou to thy place: I thought to promote thee unto great honour; but, lo, the LORD LORD hath kept thee back from honour.
12: And Balaam said unto Balak, Spake I not also to thy messengers which thou sentest unto me, saying,
13: If Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond the commandment of the LORD LORD , to do either either good or bad of mine own mind; but but what the LORD LORD saith, that will I speak?
14: And now, behold, I go unto my people: come therefore, and therefore, and I will advertise thee what this people shall do to thy people in the latter days.
15: And the man whose eyes are open hath said:
16: Falling into a trance into a trance , but having his eyes open:
17: And destroy all the children of Sheth.
19: And shall destroy him that remaineth of the city.
20: But his latter end shall be shall be that he perish for ever.
21: And thou puttest thy nest in a rock.
22: Until Asshur shall carry thee away captive.
23: Alas, who shall live when God doeth this!
24: And he also shall perish for ever.
25: And Balaam rose up, and went and returned to his place: and Balak also went his way.
